title: rubus serpens var. capitatus
intro: Rubus serpens var. capitatus, also known as the creeping raspberry or woodland raspberry, is a variety of the Rubus serpens species, a type of bramble with thorny stems and edible fruits. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus serpens var. capitatus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
